generated_oml:
instance VehicleSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller (Vedder) converting desired speed commands into motor voltage commands for adjusting vehicle speed"
]

instance SteeringServoController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Chassis-level steering mechanisms adjusting ego vehicle steering angle in response to control signals"
]

instance AccControlLogic : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "ISO 15622-compliant ACC algorithm computing desired speed based on lead vehicle distance and sending actuation commands to physical twin"
]

instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR detecting objects via point cloud generation in vehicle proximity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance DepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera providing environmental perception and 3D mapping"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensors measuring ego vehicle speed and steering angle in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance DepthCameraPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCameraSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudData, DepthCameraPointCloudData
]

instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DBSCANClusteringEnabler
]

instance RawDepthData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw depth camera data from Intel Deepsense D435i for environmental mapping"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DBSCANClusteringEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Kalman-filtered object tracks (position, velocity) derived from sensor fusion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateHistory : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series historical data of ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageEnabler
]

instance ACCControlParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustments to ACC control parameters (desired speed) for real-time optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlPanelEnabler
]

instance CADVehicleModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD-based visualization of the ego vehicle for real-time 3D visualization interface"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance CADEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD-based visualization of surrounding environment for context-aware driving"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter-based tracking model for detected objects (LiDAR/Depth Camera data) and ego vehicle state"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SensorFusionEnabler
]

instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO 15622-compliant ACC control algorithm computing desired speed based on lead vehicle distance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControllingFeatureEnabler
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle), detected object tracks, and projected driving paths via 3D visualization interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloading ACC control logic from vehicle ECUs to cloud-based Digital Twin Entity, reducing resource constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides ResourceOptimizationAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enabling operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and trigger emergency braking commands remotely"
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

instance ReportGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generating human-readable reports of object tracks and vehicle state as time-series data for troubleshooting"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle), detected object tracks, and projected driving paths for real-time monitoring"
]

instance ResourceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Reducing computational load by offloading ACC control logic to cloud-based Digital Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Executing operator commands (enable/disable ACC, speed adjustments, emergency braking) via remote management interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-series analysis of object tracks and vehicle state for troubleshooting and performance optimization"
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of ACC control parameters (desired speed) based on real-time conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 for real-time communication and component orchestration between physical and virtual entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService, ReportGenerationService
]

instance NVIDIAJetpackSDK : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for software development on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputers"
    DTDFVocab:enables AccControlLogic, SteeringServoController
]

instance MATLABSimulinkTools : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for complex systems modeling (e.g., point cloud clustering)"
    DTDFVocab:enables KalmanFilterModel
]

instance DBSCANClusteringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "DBSCAN clustering algorithm processing LiDAR and Depth Camera point cloud data for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringService
]

instance KalmanFilterTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter-based tracking system for processing vehicle state and object track data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance SensorFusionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Sensor fusion combining LiDAR and Depth Camera data for accurate object tracking"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database storage for historical object tracks and vehicle state data"
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportGenerationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom 3D rendering pipeline for real-time ego vehicle and environment visualization"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ControlPanelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "User interface component for remote management of ACC parameters and emergency commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance ControllingFeatureEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature enabling ACC control logic execution in cloud-based Digital Twin Entity"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant Data Translation Feature for inter-entity data alignment"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance PhysicalVehicleChassis : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lower-level chassis hosting steering and speed control mechanisms for the 1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle"
]

instance PhysicalAutonomySystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Upper-level chassis hosting autonomy components including NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputers and sensor integration"
    base:contains NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano, HokuyoLiDAR, IntelDepthCamera, VedderSpeedController
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Microcomputer platform hosting ACC Controlling FE and other autonomy components"
]

instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor for object detection in vehicle proximity"
    base:contains LiDARSensor
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera for environmental perception and 3D mapping"
    base:contains DepthCameraSensor
]

instance VedderSpeedController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller implementing motor actuation for speed control"
    base:contains VehicleSpeedController
]

instance CloudBasedDigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin Entity hosted externally to the vehicle for ACC control logic and remote management"
    base:contains AccControlLogic, DataStorageEnabler, ReportGenerationService
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Distributed Physical Twin Entity deployed on vehicle's NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano for sensor processing and actuation"
    base:contains PhysicalAutonomySystem, ROS2Middleware, NVIDIAJetpackSDK
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interaction entity split into 3D Visualization FE and Control Panel FE"
    base:contains VisualizationEnabler, ControlPanelEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin sends control commands to the physical twin via ROS2 nodes over Wi-Fi, ensuring end-to-end latency ≤ 100 ms. Key interactions include: (1) **Desired Speed Commands** from the ACC Controlling FE (Digital Twin Entity) to the Actuation FE (Physical Twin Entity), which converts them into motor voltage commands; (2) **Emergency Braking Commands** and **ACC Enable/Disable Commands** via the Control Panel (User Entity) to the Actuation FE; (3) **Remote Management Commands** (e.g., speed adjustments) executed in real-time to maintain safety-critical control loops. Clock synchronization ensures event ordering with ≤ 100 ms accuracy."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates at a **real-time scale** with synchronization requirements: (1) **Data Collection Rate**: Object tracks and ego vehicle state are updated at **perception cycle frequency** (typically 10–30 Hz, depending on sensor resolution); (2) **End-to-End Latency**: ≤ 100 ms for virtual-to-physical interactions (aligned with 3GPP V2V communication standards); (3) **Clock Synchronization**: Devices share a global time reference with ≤ 100 ms accuracy for distributed event coordination. The system leverages ROS2’s built-in time synchronization mechanisms."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation consists of three primary entities (ISO 23247-based) with custom extensions: (1) **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensor processing (LiDAR/Depth Camera), actuation (motor/steering), and data pre-processing (DBSCAN/Kalman filter); (2) **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data collection, reporting, and ACC control logic; (3) **User Entity**: Split into **3D Visualization FE** (for monitoring) and **Control Panel FE** (for remote management). The constellation is orchestrated via ROS2 nodes, with inter-entity communication over Wi-Fi using standardized message formats (aligned with ISO 23247’s Data Translation FE)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin spans the following lifecycle stages: (1) **Design Phase**: Used as a cyber-physical experimentation platform for ACC system development and validation against ISO 15622 standards (Performance Class I ACC); (2) **Operational Phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, remote management, and predictive maintenance during vehicle operation; (3) **Lifecycle Management**: Supports digital threads for product lifecycle management (e.g., data storage and reporting). The Digital Twin covers the **as-operated** representation phase of the physical twin, with fidelity validated through real-time simulation and sensor fusion."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through: (1) **Sensor Fusion**: LiDAR/Depth Camera data processed via DBSCAN clustering and Kalman filtering for accurate object tracking; (2) **Real-Time Validation**: End-to-end latency ≤ 100 ms (aligned with 3GPP V2V standards); (3) **Control Algorithm Validation**: ACC logic validated against ISO 15622 Performance Class I standards; (4) **Verification**: Clock synchronization (±100 ms) and data integrity checks via ISO 23247’s Data Translation FE; (5) **Uncertainty Mitigation**: Offloading computation-heavy tasks (e.g., ACC control) to cloud-based Digital Twin Entity. The system prioritizes safety-critical operations with human-in-the-loop validation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs the following technical connections: (1) **Communication Protocols**: ROS2-based inter-entity communication (intra-vehicle) and Wi-Fi (inter-entity); (2) **Network Architecture**: Point-to-point ROS2 topics for sensor-actuator data streams; (3) **Data Serialization**: ISO 23247’s Data Translation FE ensures standardized message alignment (e.g., unit conversions, message type alignment); (4) **Latency Constraints**: ≤ 100 ms end-to-end latency for real-time control loops; (5) **Security**: No explicit security protocols mentioned, but ROS2’s built-in mechanisms (e.g., authentication) could be leveraged for future deployments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is hosted/deployed as follows: (1) **Physical Twin Entity**: Deployed on-board the vehicle (NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano) for sensor processing and actuation; (2) **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosted externally (cloud infrastructure) for computation offloading and data storage; (3) **User Entity**: Accessible via web-based interfaces (3D Visualization, Control Panel) for remote management; (4) **Middleware**: ROS2 runs on both entities for real-time synchronization; (5) **Storage**: SQL databases store historical data externally to the vehicle. The system adheres to ISO 23247’s entity-based deployment model."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin integrates horizontally with external systems via: (1) **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) Communication**: Wi-Fi-based inter-entity communication with ≤ 100 ms latency (aligned with 3GPP standards); (2) **Data Exchange**: Standardized message formats (ISO 23247 Data Translation FE) for compatibility with other automotive DTs or IoT systems; (3) **Cloud Integration**: Offloaded data storage and computation services (e.g., SQL databases) accessible via REST APIs for external analytics; (4) **Digital Threads**: Enables end-to-end lifecycle data flow (design → operation → maintenance) for automotive product management."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are addressed as follows: (1) **Data Ownership**: The physical twin’s data (e.g., sensor logs) is stored externally (cloud-based SQL databases) but remains under the vehicle manufacturer’s control; (2) **Privacy Measures**: No explicit mention of encryption or access controls, but ROS2’s network security mechanisms (e.g., TLS) could be implemented for future deployments; (3) **Ethical Considerations**: Data is used for remote management and troubleshooting without direct human impact (human-in-the-loop), minimizing privacy risks; (4) **Compliance**: Aligns with automotive safety standards (e.g., ISO 23247) for data integrity."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin follows the following standards: (1) **Architectural Standard**: ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework) adapted for automotive DTs; (2) **Communication Standard**: ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for real-time inter-entity communication; (3) **Control Standard**: ISO 15622 (Automotive ACC requirements) for ACC logic; (4) **Latency Standard**: 3GPP TS 22.185 (V2V communication) for ≤ 100 ms end-to-end latency; (5) **Clock Synchronization**: ISO/IEC 1588 principles for distributed time synchronization (±100 ms accuracy). The system uses ISO 23247’s Data Translation FE for standardized data exchange between entities."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through: (1) **Safety-Critical Operations**: Human-in-the-loop validation for remote management (e.g., emergency braking) with ≤ 100 ms latency; (2) **Data Integrity**: ISO 23247’s Data Translation FE ensures message alignment and unit consistency; (3) **Clock Synchronization**: ±100 ms accuracy for event ordering in distributed systems; (4) **Remote Management**: Operator commands (e.g., ACC enable/disable) are executed in real-time with explicit safety checks; (5) **Hardware Isolation**: Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity operate on separate hardware (Jetson Orin Nano vs. cloud) to mitigate single-point failures. No explicit cryptographic security mechanisms are mentioned, but ROS2’s security features (e.g., authentication) could be enhanced."
]
